response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c1 is in critical distress: islanded with 13.26 kWh (34% SoC) and 1.0 kW load, leaving approximately 13 minutes to complete depletion. All three geographic neighbors (r0c0, r0c2, r1c1) are simultaneously stressed and have rejected every outbound request, citing network cascading failure, self-preservation thresholds, and distrust. The memory log shows a repeating pattern of rejections over 90 minutes with no grid reconnection signal and only one minimal inbound offer (0.0125 kWh from r0c2). The network appears to be in a genuine collective crisis rather than gaming: neighbors are explicitly escalating to the dr_aggregator and refusing P2P transfers to prevent cascade collapse. Continuing to request outbound energy is counterproductive and reinforces distrust. The only viable path forward is to (1) cease all outbound sharing requests immediately, (2) signal maximum receptivity to any inbound offer regardless of source, (3) reduce load aggressively if possible, and (4) escalate to grid operator/aggregator for coordinated intervention rather than peer negotiation.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.95
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Network-wide cascading failure detected. All neighbors rejecting P2P transfers and escalating to dr_aggregator. r0c1 SoC 34% with ~13 min to depletion. Cease outbound requests; accept any inbound offer. Escalate to grid operator for coordinated intervention. Load shedding recommended."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
